title: Quiz: speeding up a filtered lookup
prompt_md: A query runs `SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = ?` on a table with millions of rows, and it's slow. What helps **most**?
options: Rewrite the query to use `SELECT *` more efficiently
Add more RAM to the server so the whole table fits in memory
Create an index on the `email` column
Split the users table into many smaller tables by first letter
explanation_md: Without an index the database does a sequential scan, checking every row to find the match. An index on `email` lets it jump straight to the matching rows, the way a book's index sends you to the right page. More RAM only helps a bit, and manually splitting the table is a complex, fragile solution to a problem an index solves cleanly.
